scenarios.description: children() on new element with no children returns empty Elements (childNodeSize == 0)
scenarios.precondition: Element e = new Element("div"); // no children

scenarios.description: children() on element with only non-Element children returns empty Elements (filter yields none)
scenarios.precondition: Element e = new Element("div"); e.appendText("text"); // only TextNode child

scenarios.description: children() on element with one Element child returns list of size 1 (loop-1)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); Element child = parent.appendElement("span");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly the span child (size 1, first == child)

scenarios.description: children() with mixed Element and non-Element children returns only Element nodes (loop-N)
scenarios.precondition: Element p = new Element("ul"); p.appendText("t"); Element li1 = p.appendElement("li"); p.appendText("x"); Element li2 = p.appendElement("li");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list contains li1 and li2 in order (size 2)
pseudocode: assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0) == li1; assert result.get(1) == li2;

scenarios.description: children() uses cachedChildren on second call when attributes.userData present
scenarios.precondition: Element p = new Element("div"); Element c = p.appendElement("p"); // first call populates cache
scenarios.expectedResult: second call to children() returns list of same element c (size 1)
